title: GAO-04-121: SSA Disability — DDS Examiner Recruitment, Retention, and Strategic Workforce Planning (2004)
description: U.S. Government Accountability Office report GAO-04-121 (published Jan 2004) examining the challenges state Disability Determination Services (DDS) face in retaining and recruiting disability examiners, the extent of DDS workforce planning, and SSA's handling of human-capital challenges. Recommends a nationwide DDS strategic workforce plan, uniform minimum examiner qualifications, and training-gap closure, with later implementation status. Directly relevant to 509 DDS examiner staffing, certification, and pay/retention issues.

source.title: SSA Disability: Improved Processes and Better Planning Could Strengthen DDS Examiner Recruitment and Retention (GAO-04-121)

notes: Authoritative: a published GAO report (product GAO-04-121; published and publicly released Jan 27, 2004) mirrored from gao.gov. The most directly DDS-workforce-relevant report in this set: it addresses examiner recruiting/retention, minimum qualifications, certification, and DDS parent-agency salary/hiring constraints. The source 'title' approximates GAO's catalog title; verify exact wording against gao.gov if needed. Single publication year so temporal_coverage start==end (2004). Recommendation table is narrative, not a declared column schema.
